summary: Plan 07 is executable after Plan 06 and Plan 08A: it now lands an unregistered bounded foundation followed by one atomic complete aws_s3 sink registration, extends the standalone aws extra with Jinja2, protects cumulative writes with IfNoneMatch then confirmed-ETag IfMatch, fails closed on ambiguous/audit-uncertain outcomes, and proves behavior through botocore plus real S3.

review_basis.head: aa71969808ee72690cd144f0de34fe26ff7511d9
review_basis.baseline: Current BaseSink and Azure sink contracts, plugin discovery and metadata gates, Plan 06 shared client contract, Plan 08 endpoint policy, botocore PutObject IfNoneMatch/IfMatch model, and Plans 03/10/12 handoffs.

warnings.detail: Implementation is not ready now. Filigree elspeth-74717426b7 remains blocked by Plan 06 elspeth-7fe6aa531f and Plan 08A elspeth-c0103e6c88; those live edges override document numbering.
Generic post-dispatch provider failure is outcome-unknown. The plan poisons the sink and stops rather than guessing or retrying through uncertain remote state.
The sink supports single-object PutObject only, bounded to 1 GiB, with no multipart upload or resume.

The repository currently lacks Wardline trust decorators. Exit-zero scanning is required but explicit config, key, provider, audit, lifecycle, and redaction tests remain the load-bearing boundary evidence.

resolved_findings.resolution: The plan prominently distinguishes review order from executable order and records its exact live Filigree blockers.
Task 1 contains no BaseSink subclass or registration; the complete class, upload, audit, lifecycle, catalog, hash, and tests register atomically in Task 2.
Plan 07 adds Jinja2 to the standalone aws extra, regenerates the final 02-to-06-to-07 lock, and proves all-extras plus isolated base/aws postures.

AWSS3SinkConfig declares the sink component type, required schema inheritance, concrete descriptions, strict forbidden-key behavior, and bounded bucket/template/rendered-key/region/endpoint validation.
Serialization uses an 8 MiB rolling spool, incremental hash/checksum, 64 KiB writes, an object-byte cap, and a per-record character cap rather than whole-object bytes.
Tier-2 row faults divert with static reasons while cumulative object-cap failure aborts the candidate write without silently dropping a boundary-crossing row.
First overwrite-protected writes use only IfNoneMatch star; later cumulative writes use only the last confirmed ETag through IfMatch, with no unconditional fallback.
Missing or malformed ETags, transport ambiguity, malformed responses, and audit uncertainty poison the instance and prevent unsafe reuse.
Provider errors become normalized static exceptions outside raw handlers; endpoint, credential, body, message, cause, and context leakage is explicitly regression-tested.
Success and failure call records share audit-primary handling, propagate existing Tier-1 errors, and convert other recorder failures to redacted cause-free AuditIntegrityError.
Close marks closed, detaches the client, resets all state, closes exactly once, and returns only a static sanitized failure if cleanup raises.
CLI and web-gate fixtures now include required schema, source/output fields, nodes, edges, metadata, version, settings, generator mock, and explicit-null acceptance.
Plugin version, assistance hints, determinism boundary, sink count, catalog golden, options metadata, component type, and final source hash land in the atomic registration slice.
A real boto3/botocore Stubber lane verifies request-model parameters, conditions, checksums, ETags, modeled 412/409 errors, default credentials, and lifecycle without network.
Plan 12 now runs a default-chain real-S3 sink lane covering every format, first-create, cumulative conditional update, fresh-sink collision, intervening-writer non-clobber, and mandatory cleanup.

Final gates include frozen lock, all-extras and forced-missing isolated imports, focused tests, Ruff, format, mypy, options/component/hash checks, diff check, Wardline, rehash-on-correction, and exact staging.
Plans 00, 03, 10, and 12 consistently assign final aws/Jinja lock ownership to Plan 07 and prove the complete dependency set in doctor, lean-image, and closeout gates.

verification: Loomweave fresh index used before source inspection; BaseSink, AzureBlobSink, assistance, discovery, and fixture anchors reconciled
botocore 1.43.46 local service model verified PutObject supports both IfNoneMatch and IfMatch and returns ETag
Plan 06 shared-client and Plan 08 endpoint policy contracts reconciled
Plans 00, 03, 10, and 12 lock, dependency, image, IAM, and live-S3 handoffs reconciled
stale skeleton, NotImplemented, raw-message, unsafe staging, fake-only residual, and lock-owner claims searched
three independent post-repair specialist reviews reached GO with no remaining Plan-07 blocker

review_history.summary: Initial review found two mergeable broken registration commits, incomplete config metadata and fixtures, missing standalone Jinja packaging, fake-only conditional-write evidence, unconditional cumulative overwrites, provider and recorder leakage, non-idempotent close, ambiguous remote-state claims, unbounded serialization, unsafe staging, and missing static/Wardline/live handoffs.
